ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT
Off Calendar Aug-20-2019 Continued To Oct-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught appeared by telephone for Union Bank.
Timothy Chambers for Sarah Stuurman and Andrew Wolfe.

The Court stated that it is not clear as to why the Parties can not work on how to resolve this based on the accountings that have been exchanged not withstanding that not all of the accountings have been filed, that it looks like the objections have to do with fees, asks why the issue of fees can't be reserved to the end of the accountings and informed Counsel that it is not going to rule on the accountings more than one at a time. Mr. Vaught stated that he tried to file the accountings, but they were rejected by the Clerk, that the accountings also affect other Beneficiaries other than Mr. Chambers', and that he is talking settlement with Mr. Chambers, but he is concerned that they will have to be back before the Court with other accountings and another petition for approval of a settlement agreement. The Court once again stated that it will not decide or rule on multiple accountings. Mr. Vaught stated that they can continue to work on settlement, that they can return to mediation and that they can have the accountings brought into mediation. Mr. Chambers stated that they are making progress on settlement, acknowledged that the ball has been in his Court for some time, stated that he was working out some issues and that he is confident that they will settle. The Petition for Relief from Breach of Trust and Breach of Fiduciary Duty, etc., the Petition for Approval of Trust Accounts, etc. and the Petition for Approval of QTIP Trust Accounts, etc. are continued to October 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught appeared by telephone for Union Bank (Petitioner).
Timothy Chambers appeared by telephone for Sarah Stuurman and Andrew Coates (Respondents).

The Court noted that it now has an accounting for the parent trust and the QTIP trust. Mr. Chambers stated that they have objections to both accountings, that he filed a formal objection to the QTIP trust accounting, that Mr. Vaught has provided additional information, that he thinks they are close to resolving the matter and that they have agreed to a 60-day continuance. Mr. Vaught stated that he has no disagreement with what Mr. Chambers is proposing. The Petition for Relief for Breach of Trust, etc., Petition for Approval of Trust Account and Petition for Approval of QTIP are continued to August 20, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to meet and confer as to how to get this to resolution. A joint status statement is to be filed by August 13, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Off Calendar May-23-2018 Continued To Oct-10-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jon Vaught for Union Bank
Timothy Chambers for Andrew Coates Wolfe and Sarah Stuurman (Petitioners) via CourtCall

Mr. Vaught stated that the matter is not resolved, but parties are working diligently, have met and conferred, and they are tearing up the existing accounting. Mr. Vaught also stated that they have hired an outside accounting firm, are starting over with an accounting to correct errors in the prior accounting, and will file a notice of proposed action after they get approval of the accounting from Mr. Chamber's clients. Mr. Vaught further stated that the parties would continue with mediation if there were still problems with the accounting, there is an estate that owns property in Italy, and requested a 120-day continuance. Mr. Chambers stated that he concurred with Mr. Vaught's remarks, and they are not in a position to wrap things up without an accounting. The Petition for Relief from Breach of Trust, etc. is continued to October 10, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust And Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For Order Compelling Account
Off Calendar Jan-31-2018 Continued To Mar-14-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Timothy Chambers for Andrew Wolfe and Sarah Stuurman (Petitioners).
Jon Vaught appeared by telephone for MUFG Union Bank.

Mr. Chambers stated that they would like nothing more than to resolve the matter, that they need some information from Union Bank, that they were expecting a petition for instructions regarding how administration expenses were paid and that instead Union Bank filed a notice of proposed action. Mr. Vaught stated that his Client elected to do a notice of proposed action, and that his Client is waiting for the 45-days to run (Feb. 11, 2018) and then will be dismissing their petition. Mr. Chambers stated that payment from the QTIP takes care of 75% of the issues, that the notice of proposed action does not address what was paid as to the QTIP and as to the Trust, and made a request to get numbers for reimbursement of the expenses. The Petition for Relief from Breach of Trust, etc. is continued to March 14, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 on the assumption that February 11th will resolve things.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Mr. Chambers stated that this gets them 75% towards resolution, that he thinks they can reach resolution on their own and requested a 60-day continuance to resolve the matter and reach settlement. The Petition for Relief from Breach of Trust, etc. is continued to January 31, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust And Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For Order Compelling Account
Off Calendar Jun-21-2017 Continued To Aug-16-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jon Vaught for Union Bank; no appearance for petitioner.

Mr. Vaught reported that he has not heard from Mr. Chambers as to what he intends to do now to resolve the matter. He stated that he had made a proposal to Mr. Chambers requesting prepayment of administrative expenses for the anticipated lifetime of beneficiary Tina High, but has as yet received no response to that proposal. He had previously been advised by Mr. Chambers of his intention to bring a petition for surcharge and to conduct discovery, but has heard nothing further and no discovery has been served. He said that a one day mediation session was held before Bette Epstein, but no resolution was reached. He agreed to contact Mr. Chambers and make a further effort to ascertain his intentions and confer on furhter actions to bring the matter to resolution, and requested a continuance of the matter. The matter was continued to August 16, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Petition To Return Trust Assets
Off Calendar Jul-06-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jon Vaught (via court call) for Union Bank. Katherine Fowler for (respondents) Sarah Stuurman & Andrew Wolfe.

The Court stated that it had been told that the parties were on track to settle. Mr. Vaught stated that they provided an accounting to the respondents and that he received an email stating that it was not the accounting that they wanted. Ms. Fowler stated that an accounting of the QTIP trust account is what they want. The Court stated that the question is what do we do here and that a petition to return assets is what is before it. Mr. Vaught stated that they are satisfied, that they have received the money and that they have provided an accounting. The Court stated that it thought that Mr. Vaught had the only surviving petition, that it thought the petition for surcharge had been withdrawn. Mr. Vaught stated that he was not aware that it had been withdrawn, that his issue has been resolved and that the other side is not satisfied. Ms. Fowler stated that the issue is Union Bank demanding $200,000.00 in additional fees but that there has been no accounting as to what has happened. The Court stated that Mr. Vaught can withdraw his petition and this will be over and the parties agreed. The Petition to Return Assets is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jon Vaught for the petitioner. Tim Chambers for (respondents) Sarah Stuurman and Andrew Wolfe.

Mr. Chambers reported that they have stalled, that they request that the petitioner make an offer on negotiations and that they need to proceed with discovery. The Court asked if the parties were willing to attend mediation and both agreed. Mr. Vaught stated that they only needed a 1-2 hour hearing on expenditures. Mr. Chambers stated that there were also issues with the conduct of the trustee and that there is less than $300,000.00 involved in the dispute. MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jon Vaught for (Petitioner) Union Bank. Timothy Chambers for (Respondents) Sara Stuurman & Andrew Coates-Wolfe.

The Court stated that the respondents are willing to return the assets, that the petitioner is seeking administrative expenses but they are not itemized and that the basic relief has been agreed to. Mr. Chambers stated that additional allegations have been raised and that they are open to resolving them informally. Mr. Vaught stated that the claim for surcharge is not an offset. Mr. Chambers stated that global resolution makes sense, that there is a question of determining dividends and that everything should be resolved at once. The Court requested that Mr. Vaught draft an order limited to the return of shares distributed, that it will set a date for a continued hearing on the other issues and that the parties should talk about resolving the remaining issues. The Petition to Return Trust Assets was continued to June 23, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lopes. (Not Reported)
